chunk_summary: Daily micronutrient anchors for Indian infants and young children (6–24 months) provide practical dietary diversity goals to prevent micronutrient gaps, based on ICMR-NIN 2020 recommendations.
learning_objectives: Understand daily micronutrient requirements for Indian infants and young children (6–24 months).
Learn practical dietary strategies to meet iron, zinc, calcium, vitamin A, and vitamin D needs.
Explore culturally appropriate food options and supplementation plans for micronutrient gaps.
Apply dietary diversity goals using the 'daily must-have anchors' approach.
key_facts: Infants 6–12 months require calcium ~300 mg/day, iron ~3 mg/day, zinc ~2.5 mg/day, vitamin A ~350 micrograms/day, and vitamin D ~400 IU/day.
Children 1–3 years require calcium ~500 mg/day, iron ~8 mg/day, zinc ~3.3 mg/day, vitamin A ~390 micrograms/day, and vitamin D ~400 IU/day.
Iron absorption improves with vitamin C-rich foods (e.g., citrus, amla, tomato, guava); avoid tea with meals.
Vitamin D is difficult to meet through diet alone; safe sunlight exposure and supplementation are recommended.
Adequate fat intake (oil, ghee, nut paste) supports energy density and absorption of fat-soluble vitamins.
reference_quote: India-relevant daily requirement values are summarized in the ICMR–NIN (2020) recommendations (also reproduced in national nutrition labelling directions).
document_key_topics: Micronutrient requirements for infants and young children
Dietary diversity strategies to prevent nutrient gaps
Culturally appropriate food options for Indian families
Vitamin D supplementation and sunlight exposure
